title: Adjust name of Podman CNI network bridge
body: Both Podman and CRI-O set up CNI bridges with the name 'cni0'. If both our CNI conflist and the CRI-O conflist are installed, whoever runs first will win - that is, they will configure the bridge, and everyone will use it. Problem: the CRI-O CNI config conflicts with ours and results in containers with no networking. Solution: rename our bridge so we don't conflict with CRI-O.

comments.body: CNI also has the option to change the cni network default (effectively namespacing cni configs), which would totally work if CRI-O actually did it, but I left https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/pull/2662 in the dust :)

I may need you to change this to cni0-podman, I would rather I didn't have to ask that but there's a narrow use case in CRI-O where we need to filter ip addresses exposed with CNI, and I was using the convention cni* to do so https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/pull/2662

note: I want to find a better solution to that but keeping that convention will help me in case a better solution can't be found
I'll do it, but I'll note that I don't think this is a sane assumption for CRI-O to make... We don't have a monopoly on the use of CNI.
haha I am very aware it's a poor assumption to make :smile:
@haircommander I think that default network actually doesn't help us - they're both trying to set up `cni0` on the host.
@haircommander Can you use `cni[0-9]+` instead of cni* for matching networks there?
oh I thought they were conflicting with each other in CNI land, not on the host. yeah you're right
I really just use strings.Prefix(NAME_OF_BRIDGE, cni)
I need to figure out if this is really something I need to worry about though.
LGTM
@mheon Will this cause any update issues?  There is nothing recorded in CNI?
Answer seems to be yes - new containers go in a new bridge, old containers stay in the old bridge.

LGTM
potential update issues are the only hedge, but that apparently look OK.
I think we need update testing here. Will investigate tomorrow.

Throwing a hold on this for further testing
/hold
According to https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1729603 we need to change the default subnet from the one that CRI-O uses, lets get that change in and get this merged.
For reference, it seems like the changes don't take effect until after a restart - so I suspect this will not break existing systems.

/hold cancel

Changed subnet over to not conflict with CRI-O.
Test failures are due to tests still using 10.88.x.x IP ranges. Oops. Will fix.
/hold
dont merge this until we get a chance to talk ... turns out podman is using some deprecated (but still supported) ipam options.  we should fix this when you get back.
Any movement on this?
yes, @mheon and i will talk today about more broad changes to eliminate deprecated configurations that we are still using.

/hold cancel

Should be good to go

We're still using the same IP address range as the CRI-O bridge, of note. This presents a conflict, but we can't fix this without breaking existing installations using static IPs. We can discuss fixing this later.

Success is green, though.
@rhatdan @baude @TomSweeneyRedHat @vrothberg @giuseppe PTAL
LGTM, assuming happy tests.

/lgtm
/hold
hold cancel if you can get tests to pass.
/hold cancel
I'm going to give Tide five minutes here, then force-merge on the assumption that the Cirrus flake blew things up.

@mheon late to the party here, but this PR did perk my ears up about something networking and specific to out test environment:  [Google automatically allocates ranges of private IPs and maintains routers for every region (data-center location). ](https://console.cloud.google.com/networking/networks/list?project=udica-247612&organizationId=54643501348) Those addresses are all routable, and therefor could cause conflicts if they ever show up to any host device (like a bridge).  I'll open an experimental PR to see if we can simply hard-code those into a CNI config installed for testing, so those networks are never re-used.
https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3892
